name: Luxe Poolside Escape
subtitle: Beach Walk & 2 Terraces
tagline: Luxury living steps from the azure sea
welcomeMessage: Welcome to the Luxe Poolside Escape! We're thrilled to host you in this beautifully designed retreat. Styled for comfort and elegance, this home is your private sanctuary on the Mediterranean. Everything has been prepared with care — dive into the pool, sip coffee on the terrace, and let the island work its magic. We're always a message away if you need anything at all.

address.nearestLandmark: Behind AVLIDA Hotel, Tombs of the Kings area

wifi.notes: If you experience connection issues, flip the switch on the socket next to the router to restart it. If the problem persists, let us know.

checkin.keyLocation: Two key boxes on the left wall, under Post Box 'D6' — open the one on the right
steps: Walk to the pool area at the back of the King's Park complex
Pass the pool and turn right along the pool's fence
Turn left at the first stone arch passage
Find the two key boxes on the left wall, under the Post Box labelled 'D6'
Open the key box on the right using code 9006
Go through the corridor — the apartment is the first door on the left (ground floor)
The entrance door is white
Once inside, place the metallic key in the key plug next to the door to switch on the electricity (just like in hotels)
Return the key to the box when you check out and reset the code
checkin.notes: If you have any trouble finding us, call or WhatsApp anytime — we're happy to guide you step by step.

gettingHere.byCar: The Google Maps link above is accurate and will navigate you directly inside the complex, all the way to the pool area and parking. You can park anywhere you like within the complex. Once you arrive, follow the step-by-step photo guide below to reach the apartment — see photos 4 onwards.
gettingHere.parking: Drive down to the pool area where the road ends. Parking spaces are freely available — park anywhere you like. The apartment entrance is within a 1-minute walk. On windy days, avoid parking under the trees for obvious reasons! 🌳
gettingHere.publicTransport: From Paphos Airport, take Bus 612 directly to the Tombs of the Kings bus station — a 10-minute walk from the apartment. From the stop, you can follow the Google Maps link above or head straight toward the AVLIDA Hotel. Walk past the hotel, turn right at the first street (at the Esso gas station), and you'll find the entrance to the King's Park complex behind it.

directionPhotos.caption: Turn right at the Esso gas station (into Morphou street)
Go up around 50m to the big construction on the right
Keep going, just a bit more...
At this construction, turn right into the narrow street
Park here or continue on foot past the pool
Follow the road to the back where the pool area is
Turn left at the first stone arch passage
Passing the pool, turn right along the pool's fence
Two key boxes under Post Box 'D6' — open the one on the right. Code: 9006
First door on the left (ground floor) — the white door is yours!

houseRules: No smoking inside — balconies and terraces only
No parties, events or loud music — quiet hours 10pm to 8am
No pets allowed on the furniture or in the pool
Please use pool towels (in the sun lounger basket) — not the bathroom towels
Pool area: absolutely no glass, no diving from the edge
Keep air conditioning doors and windows closed when in use
Any breakages must be reported — we understand accidents happen
Please sort rubbish into the recycling bins provided

appliances.name: Electricity
appliances.instructions: To have electricity, place the metallic key in the key plug on the wall next to the door — just like in hotels. Remember to remove it and take your keys with you every time you leave the apartment.

appliances.name: Kitchen
appliances.instructions: The kitchen includes an electric stove, electric oven, a basic coffee maker and grinder. You're welcome to use all of them — please clean each appliance after use. Basic cooking ingredients are provided (salt, pepper, olive oil) and coffee beans — help yourself! Important: Cyprus's warm weather can attract insects. Please clean dishes promptly, dispose of food scraps immediately, and make sure nothing is left on the counter or dropped on the floor.

appliances.name: Washing Machine
appliances.instructions: You'll find the washing machine in the kitchen. The Quick Wash setting works well for most loads. If you have any questions, don't hesitate to reach out!

appliances.name: Dishwasher
appliances.instructions: The dishwasher is available for your convenience — tabs are provided under the sink. We recommend using the Eco program: it's energy-efficient and works perfectly for everyday dishes.

appliances.name: Shower & Hot Water
appliances.instructions: It usually takes 2–3 minutes for the water to get warm — this is normal. If the water doesn't warm up, there may not have been enough sun for the solar panels that day. In that case, press the heating button next to the bathroom door and wait 20 minutes. Important: turn off the heating system before stepping into the shower. (This is only needed around 10% of the time — Cyprus is usually very sunny! ☀️)

appliances.name: Terraces & Umbrella
appliances.instructions: The apartment has two terraces — feel free to set them up as you like. Outdoor cushions and décor are stored inside and can be arranged on the terraces. For the large patio umbrella: do not use it in windy conditions, close it at night or when not in use, and make sure it is securely closed and tied before checkout.

appliances.name: Pool Access
appliances.instructions: The pool key is on the key chain with your apartment keys. The shared pool is usually quiet — please be mindful of other guests and observe any rules posted in the pool area. Use the beach towels provided in the apartment for the pool, not the bathroom towels.

appliances.name: Bathroom
appliances.instructions: Fresh towels, a hair dryer, and basic toiletries are provided. If you need anything extra, just let us know. Important: Cyprus's plumbing cannot handle toilet paper — please use the bin next to the toilet instead. Thank you!

appliances.name: Drinking Water
appliances.instructions: Tap water in Cyprus is generally safe, but many guests prefer bottled water. Large bottles are available at any supermarket nearby.

appliances.name: Plants
appliances.instructions: You'll find a few plants around the apartment — they help keep the space feeling natural and cozy. If you're staying for more than a few days, we'd really appreciate a water every 3–4 days. Thank you! 🌿

items.name: Venus Beach
items.tip: Sandy beach perfect for swimming and sunbathing. Famous for some of the most beautiful sunsets on the whole island!

items.name: Faros (Lighthouse) Beach
items.tip: Calm atmosphere with sun beds and umbrellas. Crystal-clear waters — great for families and quiet days by the sea.

items.name: Alykes Beach
items.tip: Lively beach with easy access to cafes and restaurants along the waterfront.

items.name: White River Beach
items.tip: A hidden gem with stunning natural beauty. Unique zen stone art installations scattered around make this place truly special.

items.name: Tombs of the Kings
items.tip: UNESCO World Heritage site. Ancient tombs carved from solid rock — a unique look at Paphos's rich history. Free to explore the area.

items.name: Paphos Castle & Harbor (Limani)
items.tip: Historic castle right by the harbour — lovely for exploring and waterfront dining.

items.name: Harbor Promenade
items.tip: Follow the promenade from the Castle along the Limani for scenic views, shops and dining. Especially charming at sunset. 🌅

items.name: Blue Lagoon (Akamas)
items.tip: Breathtaking turquoise waters — ideal for snorkelling and swimming. Take a boat trip from Latchi harbour.

items.name: Paphos Wreck (MV Demetrios II)
items.tip: Shipwreck visible from shore near Chloraka. A favourite for photographers — adds a touch of maritime mystery to the coastline.

items.name: Aphrodite's Rock (Petra tou Romiou)
items.tip: The legendary birthplace of Aphrodite. Iconic rock formation with fantastic photo opportunities.

items.name: Akamas Peninsula
items.tip: Nature reserve ideal for hiking, with the Blue Lagoon accessible by foot trail or boat.

items.name: Troodos Mountains
items.tip: Stunning mountain scenery, great for hiking and exploring traditional villages. A world apart from the coast.

items.name: Wine Tasting
items.tip: Nearby wineries offer tastings and tours. Try Vouni Panayia Winery for an authentic local wine experience.

items.name: Avakas Gorge
items.tip: Scenic 2–3 hour hike through impressive rock formations and diverse flora. Best enjoyed early in the morning.

items.name: Kremiotis Waterfall
items.tip: Hidden waterfall near Kritou Terra — peaceful hike through lush surroundings. Especially beautiful after the rainy season.

items.name: Aphrodite & Adonis Nature Trails
items.tip: Interconnected trails with breathtaking coastal views and views of the Blue Lagoon below. Start near the Baths of Aphrodite. Moderately challenging.

eatAndDrink.name: O Firis
eatAndDrink.tip: Modern twist on Cypriot cuisine with generous portions at great prices. Live music almost every evening. Very popular — book ahead!

eatAndDrink.name: Georgia
eatAndDrink.tip: Very local and authentically Cypriot family-run restaurant. Simple, honest food at great prices.

eatAndDrink.name: Three Little Pigs
eatAndDrink.tip: Local favourite with high-quality food, great service, and a lovely atmosphere. Their house wine is a treat!

eatAndDrink.name: Psarou Fish Tavern
eatAndDrink.tip: Cozy tavern known for fresh fish, traditional meze, and a friendly local vibe.

eatAndDrink.name: Kingfisher Taverna
eatAndDrink.tip: Small, relaxed taverna serving high-quality fish dishes and local favourites in a more upscale setting.

eatAndDrink.name: West End Hotel & Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Family-run with generous portions and delicious seafood — steps from the sea.

eatAndDrink.name: Lidl
eatAndDrink.tip: Budget-friendly with a wide range of groceries. Great for stocking up on basics.

eatAndDrink.name: AlphaMega
eatAndDrink.tip: Large supermarket with a wide variety of fresh produce and local Cypriot items.

shopping.name: Kings Avenue Mall
shopping.tip: The main shopping mall in Paphos. Wide range of shops, restaurants and a cinema.

shopping.name: Paphos Market
shopping.tip: Covered marketplace with stalls for clothing, bags, jewelry, beach gear and local souvenirs.

shopping.name: Old Town
shopping.tip: Wander through the beautiful Old Town and discover many small boutique stores and artisan shops.

gettingAround.name: Bus
gettingAround.info: The central bus station (Tombs of the Kings) is a 10-min walk away, with direct connections to main attractions, the airport, and nearby towns.

gettingAround.name: RideNow (Car Sharing)
gettingAround.info: Car sharing works well around Paphos, including to and from the airport. Download the app, sign up in 5 minutes. Cars typically available within a 5–10 min walk.

gettingAround.name: Bolt (Taxi)
gettingAround.info: Budget-friendly taxi app. Recommended for getting around Paphos without the uncertainty of metered cabs.

contacts.name: Emergency Services
contacts.note: Police, Ambulance, Fire — free from any phone

emergency.nearestHospital: Famagusta General Hospital — 25 min drive. Follow signs for Famagusta via the B6.
emergency.nearestPharmacy: Pernera Pharmacy — 7 min walk on Protaras Avenue. Open Mon–Fri 8:30am–7pm, Sat 9am–2pm.

steps: Strip the bed linens and leave them piled on the bed
Wash up or run the dishwasher one last time
Clear the fridge and dispose of any leftover food
Take all rubbish bags to the outdoor bins
Close and securely tie the patio umbrella
Return outdoor cushions and décor inside
Close all windows, doors and shutters
Switch off all lights, AC, and the TV
Remove the metallic key from the electricity plug by the door
Lock the apartment door and return all keys to the keybox — reset the code after
Drop us a WhatsApp to let us know you've left — and we'd love a review! 😊
checkout.lateCheckout: We try to be flexible — if you'd like a late checkout, please let us know 1–2 days before your departure and we'll do our best to arrange it.
